chunks.text: A Berkeley View of Systems Challenges for AI (EECS-2017-159) by Ion Stoica, Dawn Song, Raluca Ada Popa, David A. Patterson, Michael W. Mahoney, Randy H. Katz, Anthony D. Joseph, Michael Jordan, Joseph M. Hellerstein, Joseph Gonzalez, Ken Goldberg, Ali Ghodsi, David E. Culler and Pieter Abbeel. This report addresses systems challenges for artificial intelligence.

Reliable, Memory Speed Storage for Cluster Computing Frameworks (EECS-2014-135) by Haoyuan Li, Ali Ghodsi, Matei Zaharia, Scott Shenker and Ion Stoica. This technical report focuses on storage solutions for cluster computing.

Multi-Resource Fair Queueing for Packet Processing (EECS-2012-166) by Ali Ghodsi, Vyas Sekar, Matei Zaharia and Ion Stoica. This report discusses fair queueing methods for packet processing with multiple resources.

HTTP: An Evolvable Narrow Waist for the Future Internet (EECS-2012-5) by Lucian Popa, Patrick Wendell, Ali Ghodsi and Ion Stoica. This report explores HTTP's role as an evolvable narrow waist for the future internet.

Dominant Resource Fairness: Fair Allocation of Multiple Resource Types (EECS-2011-18) by Ali Ghodsi, Matei Zaharia, Benjamin Hindman, Andrew Konwinski, Scott Shenker and Ion Stoica. This report introduces a fair allocation method for multiple resource types.

Mesos: A Platform for Fine-Grained Resource Sharing in the Data Center (EECS-2010-87) by Benjamin Hindman, Andrew Konwinski, Matei Zaharia, Ali Ghodsi, Anthony D. Joseph, Randy H. Katz, Scott Shenker and Ion Stoica. This report describes Mesos as a platform for resource sharing in data centers.

Dominant Resource Fairness: Fair Allocation of Heterogeneous Resources in Datacenters (EECS-2010-55) by Ali Ghodsi, Matei Zaharia, Benjamin Hindman, Andrew Konwinski, Scott Shenker and Ion Stoica. This report addresses fair allocation of heterogeneous resources in datacenters.

Nexus: A Common Substrate for Cluster Computing (EECS-2009-158) by Benjamin Hindman, Andrew Konwinski, Matei Zaharia, Ali Ghodsi, Anthony D. Joseph, Scott Shenker and Ion Stoica. This report presents Nexus as a common substrate for cluster computing.
